content.text: Claude's permission model has two layers:
that govern what a user can do across the whole organisation, and
that control access within a specific workspace. Users can hold an org role and separate workspace roles simultaneously — for example an Org Developer who is also a workspace_admin in one workspace.
Understanding how these layers interact is essential for applying least-privilege access: granting each person the minimum permission they need, no more.

attrs.alt: Claude role hierarchy: org-level and workspace-level roles with permission breakdown

content.text: There are four roles in total: three at org level (
). An org admin has implicit access to all workspaces regardless of workspace-level role assignment.

attrs.alt: Flowchart: which role to assign to a new team member

content.text: When in doubt, assign org developer + workspace_developer. Elevate to admin or workspace_admin only when the person actively needs to manage members or keys. Broad admin grants are the most common source of accidental data exposure.
Org admin is the highest-privilege role. Assign it to IT leads, security officers, and whoever owns the Anthropic Console relationship. It grants unrestricted access to every org-level operation.
Set org policies (data retention, model access)
Always keep at least two org admins in case one loses access. The Console warns if the org drops below two admins.
Assigning org admin via the Admin API

import anthropic, os
client = anthropic.Anthropic(api_key=os.environ["ANTHROPIC_ADMIN_KEY"])

# Promote an existing member to org admin
client.admin.users.update(
    user_id="user_01abc",
    role="admin"               # "admin" | "developer" | "billing"
)

The default role for everyone who uses Claude products but does not need to manage the organisation. They can create and revoke their own API keys, use the Claude API within any workspace they belong to, and view their own usage — nothing more.
The billing role is designed for finance teams who need to see invoices and manage payment methods without any access to Claude itself. A billing user
create conversations, call the API, or view member lists.

A user can hold billing AND developer simultaneously if they need both access paths. Set org role to developer; then grant billing access separately via the Console billing settings. The Admin API sets one primary org role — billing access is additive.
a single workspace and are independent per workspace. A user can be workspace_admin in the "engineering" workspace and workspace_developer in the "data-science" workspace at the same time.
Invite existing org members into the workspace.
Remove members from the workspace (does not remove from org).
Promote/demote workspace members between workspace roles.
Create and revoke workspace-scoped API keys.
Update workspace display name and description.
Use Claude within the workspace via web, mobile, or the API key assigned to that workspace.
Create personal API keys (bound to their user identity, not the workspace).
manage other workspace members or create shared workspace keys.

# Assign a user as workspace_admin
client.admin.workspaces.members.create(
    workspace_id="wkspc_01abc",
    user_id="user_01xyz",
    workspace_role="workspace_admin"
)

# Change an existing member from developer to admin
client.admin.workspaces.members.update(
    workspace_id="wkspc_01abc",
    user_id="user_01xyz",
    workspace_role="workspace_admin"
)

# Demote back to developer
client.admin.workspaces.members.update(
    workspace_id="wkspc_01abc",
    user_id="user_01xyz",
    workspace_role="workspace_developer"
)
How Org and Workspace Roles Interact
Full org + all workspaces — workspace role is redundant
Can use Claude + manage members/keys in that workspace
Can use Claude in that workspace only
Cannot use workspace API keys; can still use Claude web if org permits
Finance access only — workspace role adds nothing practical
An org admin is implicitly a workspace_admin in every workspace. You do not need to explicitly add org admins to workspaces — they already have full access.
Onboarding a team via the Admin API

from typing import Literal

OrgRole = Literal["admin", "developer", "billing"]
WsRole  = Literal["workspace_admin", "workspace_developer"]

def onboard_member(client, email: str, org_role: OrgRole,
                   workspace_assignments: dict[str, WsRole]) -> None:
    """Invite user, then assign to each workspace."""
    # 1. Invite to org
    invite = client.admin.invites.create(email=email, role=org_role)
    print(f"Invited {email} as {org_role} (invite {invite.id})")

    # 2. Workspace assignments happen after the user accepts the invite
    #    Store intent for later reconciliation
    for ws_id, ws_role in workspace_assignments.items():
        print(f"  → will assign {ws_role} in {ws_id} after acceptance")

# Example
onboard_member(
    client,
    email="alice@company.com",
    org_role="developer",
    workspace_assignments={
        "wkspc_01engineering": "workspace_admin",
        "wkspc_02datascience":  "workspace_developer",
    }
)
Reconcile roles from a CSV manifest

import csv, anthropic, os

client = anthropic.Anthropic(api_key=os.environ["ANTHROPIC_ADMIN_KEY"])

with open("team-roles.csv") as f:
    reader = csv.DictReader(f)   # columns: email, org_role, workspace_id, workspace_role
    for row in reader:
        # Find user by email
        users = client.admin.users.list(email=row["email"]).data
        if not users:
            print(f"WARN: {row['email']} not yet a member — invite first")
            continue
        uid = users[0].id

        # Set org role
        client.admin.users.update(user_id=uid, role=row["org_role"])

        # Set workspace role
        if row.get("workspace_id"):
            client.admin.workspaces.members.create(
                workspace_id=row["workspace_id"],
                user_id=uid,
                workspace_role=row["workspace_role"]
            )
        print(f"Updated {row['email']}: org={row['org_role']}")
Run regular access reviews — at minimum quarterly — to confirm that role assignments still reflect current org structure.

def audit_roles(client) -> None:
    """Print a role summary for all org members."""
    all_users = []
    after = None
    while True:
        page = client.admin.users.list(limit=100, **({'after': after} if after else {}))
        all_users.extend(page.data)
        if not page.has_more:
            break
        after = page.last_id

    print(f"{'Email':<35} {'Org Role':<15} {'Created'}")
    print("-" * 65)
    for u in sorted(all_users, key=lambda x: x.role):
        print(f"{u.email:<35} {u.role:<15} {u.created_at[:10]}")

    admins = [u for u in all_users if u.role == "admin"]
    if len(admins) < 2:
        print(f"\nWARNING: Only {len(admins)} org admin(s) — add a second!")

audit_roles(client)
Flag any admin account that hasn't logged in for 90+ days. Stale admin accounts are high-value targets for account takeover — demote or remove them during access reviews.
Security incidents involving Claude often stem from a small set of repeated misconfiguration patterns. Recognising them lets you audit proactively.

Teams in a hurry give every developer org admin to avoid "permission denied" errors. This eliminates the audit trail value — every action looks like an admin action — and means any compromised developer account can delete workspaces, modify SSO settings, or access billing.
Audit the member list quarterly. Demote anyone with admin who doesn't actively use admin capabilities. Document the business justification for each admin grant.
2. Shared service account with admin role
Some teams create one "automation" org member with admin role and share its credentials across pipelines. When that account is compromised, every pipeline is compromised simultaneously.
Use the Admin API with an
key from a secrets manager instead of a human user account for automation. Admin API keys are revocable independently and leave audit log entries with the key ID.

Finance contractors receive billing role access during an audit and are never offboarded. Billing accounts cannot use Claude, so no one notices them — but they can see all usage data, which may be sensitive.
Set a time-limited reminder when granting billing access to contractors. Include billing role accounts in your quarterly access review.

In small teams it feels easier to make everyone a workspace_admin so anyone can add a new colleague without asking IT. In practice this means any workspace member can create workspace-scoped API keys — which are shared credentials with elevated blast radius.
Keep workspace_admin to team leads and designated deputies. Use the SCIM group push or a nightly sync script for member additions rather than ad-hoc self-service.

The Admin API does not send email notifications when roles change. If your security policy requires notification of privilege escalations, instrument your automation code to send alerts via your preferred channel when the org role is set to

import anthropic, os, smtplib
from email.message import EmailMessage

client = anthropic.Anthropic(api_key=os.environ["ANTHROPIC_ADMIN_KEY"])

def promote_to_admin_with_alert(user_id: str, approver_email: str) -> None:
    # Fetch user info before change
    user = client.admin.users.retrieve(user_id=user_id)

    # Apply promotion
    client.admin.users.update(user_id=user_id, role="admin")

    # Send notification
    msg = EmailMessage()
    msg["Subject"] = f"[Claude] Admin role granted to {user.email}"
    msg["From"]    = "security-alerts@company.com"
    msg["To"]      = ["security@company.com", approver_email]
    msg.set_content(
        f"User {user.email} (ID: {user_id}) was promoted to org admin.\n"
        f"Approved by: {approver_email}\n"
        f"Timestamp: {__import__('datetime').datetime.utcnow().isoformat()}Z"
    )
    with smtplib.SMTP("localhost") as s:
        s.send_message(msg)

    print(f"Promoted {user.email} to admin; security team notified")
Use this checklist when reviewing your Claude role configuration:
— every admin is a named individual.
granted only to finance — not to developers who also need billing visibility.
limited to team leads (typically one per workspace).
comparing Admin API member list against current HRIS.
removes members from org and all workspaces within minutes of IdP deprovisioning.
in a risk register with business justification and review date.
monthly to verify that role changes are expected and authorised.
Claude's permission model uses four roles across two layers. At the org level,
is for finance-only access. Within each workspace,

uses the workspace. Apply least privilege by defaulting to developer + workspace_developer and elevating only when genuinely needed. Automate role assignment with the Admin API for repeatable, auditable provisioning, and run quarterly access reviews to keep the permission matrix aligned with actual org structure.
